title: Flaky German Chicken Tagine
description: A garlicky, cumin‑spiced chicken tagine with crispy, flaky skin, served over low‑carb cauliflower for a crowd‑pleasing German twist.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a heavy‑bottomed tagine or Dutch oven over medium heat and toast the cumin seeds until fragrant.
Add the chicken thighs skin side down and sear until the skin is golden and crisp, then flip and sear the other side for a minute.
Stir in the minced garlic, sliced carrots, and halved onion, cooking until the vegetables start to soften, about 3 minutes.
Incorporate the tomato paste, stirring to coat the chicken and vegetables evenly.
Pour in enough water to come halfway up the sides of the chicken, bring to a gentle simmer, and cover the pot.
Reduce the heat to low, let the mixture simmer for 25 minutes or until the chicken is tender and the sauce thickens.
Add the cauliflower florets and spring onions, cover again, and cook for another 10 minutes until the cauliflower is tender but still firm.
Serve the chicken and vegetables hot, spooning the aromatic sauce over the top and garnishing with fresh herbs if desired.
